Bug / Task Description: Character-class range matching in the glob engine is off at the top of each range: a bracket range like [a-z] fails to match its own upper-bound character (e.g. 'z' is not matched, [0-9] does not match '9'). Ranges must be inclusive of both endpoints. Fix the range membership test so the upper-bound character of a class range matches.
Commit Subject: fix(glob): character class ranges must include the upper bound
